text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听BBC Learning English的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Bekka. How many of our listeners, Neil, do you think love chocolate?
zh: 我是Neil。我是Bekka。Neil，你觉得我们的听众里有多少人喜欢巧克力？

text: Well, I can't think of anyone who doesn't love chocolate.
zh: 我想不出有谁不喜欢巧克力的。

text: I know I certainly do. Yes, I thought you might say that, having seen your mid -morning snack most days.
zh: 我知道我肯定喜欢。是啊，我猜你会这么说，毕竟我几乎每天都看到你吃上午茶点。

text: That's true. I do love a bit of dark chocolate. Do you know, I really like the one that's got some chilli in it.
zh: 没错。我确实很喜欢黑巧克力。你知道吗，我特别喜欢那种加了辣椒的。

text: What's your favourite, Becca? Oh, I also love dark chocolate, but chilli's a bit spicy for me.
zh: 那你最喜欢什么口味呢，Becca？哦，我也喜欢黑巧克力，不过辣椒对我来说有点太刺激了。

text: I like it with sea salt. Mmm, that's also good. But now to the stuff that makes chocolate possible.
zh: 我喜欢加海盐的那种。嗯，那个也很好吃。不过现在我们来聊聊让巧克力得以存在的原料。

text: Cocoa, a key ingredient, and it's become more expensive in recent years.
zh: 可可，是巧克力的关键原料，近年来价格一直在上涨。

text: Now, a new EU law could make chocolate even more expensive.
zh: 现在，一项新的欧盟法律可能会让巧克力变得更贵。

text: Today we'll talk about how this law could change things for farmers, companies and chocolate lovers,
zh: 今天我们要聊聊这项法律会如何影响农民、企业以及巧克力爱好者，

text: as discussed on the recent BBC World Service programme, Business Daily.
zh: 这也是最近BBC世界服务节目《Business Daily》讨论的话题。

text: As always, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases,
zh: 一如既往，我们会学习一些实用的新单词和短语，

text: and you'll find all the vocabulary along with a transcript on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇和文字稿。

text: Before we get started, Neil, I have a question for you.
zh: 在开始之前，Neil，我有个问题要问你。

text: Europeans are big fans of chocolate, just like us.
zh: 欧洲人和我们一样，也是巧克力的忠实粉丝。

text: Around how many tonnes of cocoa beans were imported to Europe in 2023?
zh: 你知道2023年欧洲大约进口了多少吨可可豆吗？

text: Was it… a 500 ,000 tonnes, b 2 million tonnes, or c 10 million tonnes?
zh: 是a. 50万吨，b. 200万吨，还是c. 1000万吨？

text: Well, I have no idea, but I'm just going to guess and say 500 ,000 tonnes.
zh: 嗯，我不知道，不过我就猜50万吨吧。

text: We'll find out if you're right later on. Now, that new EU law that we were talking about earlier
zh: 稍后我们会揭晓答案是否正确。好了，我们之前提到的那项新欧盟法律，

text: would stop companies buying cocoa from land where any trees were cut down in the last six years.
zh: 将禁止企业从过去六年内有树木被砍伐的土地上采购可可。

text: The process of cutting down trees is called deforestation.
zh: 砍伐树木的过程被称为“毁林”。

text: Albert Smith, who owns a small chocolate business, believes that the new law wouldn't change too much for his company.
zh: 拥有一家小型巧克力企业的Albert Smith认为，这项新法律不会给他的公司带来太大改变。

text: We have all the information on farm level already because
zh: 我们在农场层面的信息已经很齐全了，因为

text: the producers that we work with are small plantations
zh: 与我们合作的生产者都是小型种植园，

text: that either work through a co -op or social enterprise and all of our cocoa is already traced back to the plantation.
zh: 他们要么通过合作社、要么通过社会企业运作，我们所有的可可来源都已经能追溯到具体种植园。

text: Albert adds that they already know where the cocoa beans come from as they work with farmers from co -ops or social enterprises.
zh: Albert补充说，由于他们与合作社或社会企业的农民合作，他们已经知道可可豆的来源。

text: A co -op in this case is a farm owned by its workers.
zh: 这里所说的“合作社”是指由工人自己所有的农场。

text: A social enterprise is a business that wants to help the community.
zh: “社会企业”则是指以帮助社区为目标的企业。

text: Because of working with farmers who are part of these organizations, Albert says that the cocoa they buy can already be traced back to the farms.
zh: 由于与这些组织中的农民合作，Albert表示他们采购的可可已经可以追溯到具体农场。

text: To trace something back to something means to find out where something has come from. Here's Albert Smith again.
zh: “追溯”某物是指查明某物的来源。我们再来听听Albert Smith怎么说。

text: Mapping means that farmers have to actually provide the geolocations of their plantation.
zh: 绘制地图意味着农民必须提供他们种植园的地理位置信息。

text: And, you know, for some farmers that are very remote, that comes also with a challenge.
zh: 你知道，对一些身处偏远地区的农民来说，这也是个挑战。

text: Albert suggests that giving a geolocation is difficult for some farmers
zh: Albert指出，对一些农民来说，提供地理位置信息是有困难的，

text: as they live in remote places and don't all have mobile phones.
zh: 因为他们生活在偏远地区，也并非人人都有手机。

text: Geolocation is data taken from a special device which shows exactly where something is.
zh: “地理位置”是指通过特殊设备获取的数据，可以准确显示某物所在的位置。

text: And the term remote means at a large distance from places where other people live.
zh: “偏远”一词指的是与人们聚居地相距很远。

text: Because of these changes, the price of chocolate might go up even more,
zh: 由于这些变化，巧克力的价格可能会进一步上涨，

text: meaning that chocolate lovers like us could really notice the difference in our bank accounts.
zh: 这意味着像我们这样的巧克力爱好者，可能会真切地感受到钱包的变化。

text: Reporter Matthew Kenyon asked a chocolate lover on the street if they would pay more for their chocolate.
zh: 记者Matthew Kenyon在街头采访了一位巧克力爱好者，问他是否愿意为巧克力多付钱。

text: Absolutely I would do that because I think the normal chocolates are way too cheap.
zh: 我当然愿意，因为我觉得现在普通巧克力的价格实在太便宜了。

text: Definitely we will pay, we have to pay more because the money will go to
zh: 我们肯定会多付钱，也必须多付钱，因为这些钱会用于

text: the local people to give them a better life, also to tackle deforestation.
zh: 帮助当地人过上更好的生活，同时也用于应对毁林问题。

text: And he asked another chocolate lover about whether he looks at where the cocoa beans come from.
zh: 他还问了另一位巧克力爱好者，是否会关注可可豆的来源。

text: For me personally, absolutely not, out of principle, because I want them to be produced as most efficiently as possible.
zh: 对我个人来说，完全不会，这是原则问题，因为我希望生产过程尽可能高效。

text: The cost is completely irrelevant. But on principle, I want production to be as efficient as possible.
zh: 成本完全不重要。但从原则上讲，我希望生产尽可能高效。

text: Otherwise, it's a waste of everybody's time. Mixed opinions, it seems there, Becker.
zh: 否则就是浪费大家的时间。看来大家的看法各不相同，Becca。

text: Whilst one chocolate lover would pay more for chocolate to support farmers and production,
zh: 一位巧克力爱好者愿意多花钱来支持农民和生产，

text: the other doesn't worry too much about the cost, but says the production process should be as efficient as possible.
zh: 而另一位则不太在意价格，但认为生产过程应尽量高效。

text: OK, time to answer our quiz question. Earlier I asked you how many tonnes of cocoa were imported to Europe in 2023
zh: 好了，是时候公布我们的问答题答案了。之前我问你2023年欧洲进口了多少吨可可，

text: and you said 500 ,000 tonnes.
zh: 你说的是50万吨。

text: Well, Neil, I'll tell you it is 2 million tonnes!
zh: 好吧，Neil，我告诉你，正确答案是200万吨！

text: Wow. Well, I was wrong and that really is a lot.
zh: 哇，看来我猜错了，这个数字真是不少啊。

text: Between us, we've probably eaten a few tonnes already this year.
zh: 我们俩今年加起来估计也吃了不少吨巧克力了。

text: At least, Becker. I'd be interested to know what kind of chocolate our listeners enjoy.
zh: 至少如此，Becca。我很想知道我们的听众都喜欢什么样的巧克力。

text: Me too, and whether they would be happy to pay more for their favourite chocolate bar.
zh: 我也是，还想知道他们是否愿意为自己喜欢的巧克力多花点钱。

text: OK, let's recap some of the vocabulary from this programme. Deforestation is the process of cutting down trees.
zh: 好了，让我们来回顾一下这期节目中的一些词汇。“毁林”是指砍伐树木的过程。

text: We heard co -op, which is a farm owned by its workers.
zh: 我们听到了“co-op”，指的是由工人自己所有的农场。

text: A social enterprise is a business that wants to help the community.
zh: “社会企业”是指以帮助社区为目标的企业。

text: To trace something back to something is to find out where something's come from.
zh: “追溯”某物是指查明它的来源。

text: Geolocation is data taken from a device which can show you exactly where a person or thing is.
zh: “地理位置”是指通过设备获取的数据，可以准确显示某人或某物的位置。

text: And finally, remote means at a large distance from places where other people live.
zh: 最后，“偏远”是指与人们聚居地相距很远。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. But if you want to test your knowledge,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。不过如果你想检验一下自己学到的知识，

text: visit our website bbclearningenglish .com, where you'll also find a worksheet and quiz for this episode. Bye. See you next time. Goodbye.
zh: 欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com，那里还有本期节目的练习题和小测验。再见，我们下次再会，拜拜。
